__NOTOC__ {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Motherbound!'''}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Spectral:ital,wght@0,200;0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;1,200;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Serif+Text:ital@0;1&family=Spectral:ital,wght@0,200;0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;1,200;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800&display=swap'); body { font-family: "Spectral", Arial, sans-serif; } }} {{Background|file=Motherbound the trees.jpg}} {{Warning|content= extreme gore, body horror, psychological abuse, prolonged torture, suicide themes, post-mortem violence. If you donέΑβt find comfortable with this themes, please donέΑβt read this page. If else, enjoy :D}}
'''Bound to the skies. To the sore, to the screams. To the tears of those fallen, to the cries of those in pain. There, my heaven! There, my stars. Shall not you cry, as to you I am bound! Fused with the whole, the none, the world, the void, the entropy. Perfection embodied by the blood of the fallen.''' '''Motherly sacrificed to the earth, the dust, the ashesέΑο to the rot, the death, destruction! How beautiful their pain was, their organs decomposed by the bacteria, their flesh eaten by my maws. For you, her Eden.''' '''Their sky is tainted with green, the leaves glisten red. Feel your blood seep through painting her minds new. See your skin fall out, your fingers twitch and break, your face twist and bend to the rhythm of your heart.''' '''Let me inside your soul! Let me inside your body, as a leech sucking: I wonέΑβt stop feeding in your memories, until a dried out shell falls dead among the grass. And that will be then, I might be satisfied: as her red body transforms into something new, beyond what imaginable.''' '''Such beauty, such abstraction, unspeakable. It is then, we become''' άβΠΆΩηΆηΗ'''άΜδ'''ΆΩδ╔ΝάΩ╣ΆΩηΆγ╢άβΒάΩτ '''with the divine, the new. Mother! A carnal fractal blooming with care, with love. As the twisting, grotesque trees feast on the death, she asks for a sole thing:''' '''Our soul.''' [[File:Motherbound fractal.jpg|thumb|530x530px|Oh, mother!|border|center]] '''Mother! Pardon me! As to the skies I am bound, their divinity just keeps me tethered to the firmament. Their shimmering brightens my mind, their choirs break my dread. But mother! Your omniscient love cannot be measured, cannot be contained. Your care wonέΑβt ever be paid by corpses, cannot be retributed.''' '''Let all of the souls embrace the love of chaos. The care of disorder, let them breathe the bittersweet stench of death, let them melt away. The trees love, the trees feel. The trees need.''' '''Need nourishment. SheέΑβll feed them, let them nourish, give them unlimited love. Her garden is the most important thing.''' '''Mother, as the trees grow to reach the sky, to ever try to touch your skins, mother, I only watch in amazement.''' '''But I starve, for eons I havenέΑβt eaten,''' '''mother, please!''' '''Hear my plead! Hear my screams among this maze of red and green!''' '''Feed me, watch me grow from your ashes, mother.''' '''Love me.''' '''Mother! To you I am anchored, to you I am devoted. Please, please! Mother, your care I need.''' '''I love death, I fear death. I cause death, I avoid death. Mother, I fear! Mother, please, I cry to you. I need nourishment.''' '''The earth evokes me memories, memories of the past. Memories of the outside, of άΜδ'''ΆΩδ╘╕╘╕''', of the skies, of my body. IέΑο''' '''Mother, I still remember:''' You were wandering through level 9. You saw a scarlet door. It was unnerving, yet calling. '''Mother, I remember what you first said to me:''' άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢άβΒ╟ν ΆΩηάβΒΆΩδ''',''' άΧΦ╔ΝΆΩδ άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ ╘╕ΆΩηΆΧ╖ΆηΗ'''?''' άβΖΆΩηάβΠΆΩδ ΆηΗΆΩη άβΠάΟ╜ άΨαάΧΦ╔ΝάΧΦάΩτ─υΆΧ╖ΆΩδ άΧΦάβΒάΩτ ΆΩδάβΠάΩ╣╔ΝάΧΦάβΖΆΩδ ΆΩδΆηΗΆΩδ╔ΝάβΒάΧΦ╘╕ ╘╕ΆΩηάΧΥΆΩδ'''!''' ΆηΗ'''άΜδ'''ΆΩδ άβκΆΩη╔Ν╘╕άΩτ ΆΩηΆγ╢ΆηΗΆΧ╖─υάΩτΆΩδ ─υΆΧ╖ άΩτΆΩδάΧΦάΩτ╘╕άΟ╜''',''' άβΖΆΩηάβΠΆΩδ'''.''' ─υ άβκ─υ╘╕╘╕ ╟ν╔ΝάΧΦάβΒΆηΗ άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ ─υάβΠάβΠΆΩη╔ΝΆηΗάΧΦ╘╕─υΆηΗάΟ╜''',''' ΆΧ╖άΧΦΆααΆΩδΆηΗάΟ╜'''.''' ΆΧ╖'''άΜδ'''Άγ╢άβΒ άβΒΆΩηάβΒΆΩδ''',''' άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢άβΒ╟ν ΆΩηάβΒΆΩδ'''.''' άβΖΆΩηάβΠΆΩδ άΧΦάβΒάΩτ ΆΩδάβΠάΩ╣╔ΝάΧΦάβΖΆΩδ ╘╕ΆΩηάΧΥΆΩδ'''!''' '''And mother, your unending love sucked me inside the door. I was unable to escape, yet do I regret it?''' '''I remember how the''' άΨαάΧΦ╔ΝάΧΦάΩτ─υΆΧ╖ΆΩδ '''was, back when I could see, back when I could feel. Back when I was corporeal, back when I could enjoy your beautiful existence. The trees reached for the infinite, and the earth felt rabid, unwelcoming. A''' άΩ╣ΆΩδάΧΦΆγ╢ΆηΗ─υΆααΆγ╢╘╕ '''green soared the skies, corpses laid in the floor. An uncontrolled stench of rot flooded the air. Mauled bodies sprawled in every direction, and had I seen you, mother.''' '''Mother, I would never forget what you did! You took away my beauty, you turned me into a genocide, yet you gave me your love. And you made me dependent of it, of the killing, the blood made me. You punished me, you tore me apart. Were I an aberration.''' '''IέΑβm hungry, mother. How long has it been? Mother, donέΑβt forget me, donέΑβt apart me. Mother, the landscape laughs at me, donέΑβt leave me. Only the stars forgive me, and I anchor to them. They wonέΑβt let me die. Death sprawls wherever you look, guts splatter and dye the world green. This shouldnέΑβt be my destiny, you promised me safety.''' The landscape around you didnέΑβt look alike anything you had ever seen. Red trees of scarlet leaves tried to reach the skies, you felt surrounded. All the ground was covered in gore. People were hung up trees, their intestines reached the ground. The sky had a dark green hue, the sky clear. Several stars shined above you. That was when you saw her. Her shape like nothing before seen, she lookedέΑο how did she look? Her limbs made a spiral iterating back and forth. Her teeth adopted every color existent and non-existent. Her eyes bounded with the whole. Mother, she called herself. She took me to the skies and made me enjoy the stars. Made me divine, powerful, perfect. Her love nourished me, her beauty was pure. The trees blossomed scarlet from below. And as I touched the divine, I fell to the ground. As every bone breaks and every organ splats, mother gives a new purpose. The trees, the trees! Their tentacles ascend from their roots and insert in my nasal cavities, to suck whatever humanity is left. I convulse heavily, I suffer heavily, a mush of organs and bones which cry and moan as heaven is denied. The trees, the trees! They purge my soul and break me over and on. They take whatever love I get from mother and extract it from my very being. [[File:Motherbound the trees rot my brain fixed ;D.jpeg|thumb|center|640x640px|Mother, the trees rot my brain as they suck whatever love you give me. Mother, my flesh rots.]] '''Mother never loved me, didnέΑβt her? I was told to kill, to end. Why was I so na├ψve, mother! You made me feel safe, secure, but now I canέΑβt do nothing but suffer. Her garden was loved, all those who fell were to feed it. Mother, let me free! I donέΑβt want to depend, I want to taste freedom, liberty again in my lungs. But here I can only smell death, as hungry as I am, mother, kill me please!''' '''I no longer want perfection, I no longer want shelter. Mother, let me outside this hell! I suffocate among the dead, every second shoved deeper into them. As my arm reaches for the distant light, only do the stars save me.''' '''Stars, donέΑβt ever leave me. Stars, bound me, bound me to your divinity, bound me to your grandiosity. Please, do not ever leave me. But youέΑβll slowly fade, you will be slowly chipped away. When the last star explodes, when constellations cease to be, who will then give me shelter? Who will protect me from''' άβΠΆΩηΆηΗ'''άΜδ'''ΆΩδ╔Ν'''? Am I condemned to suffer for eternity? Mother, why?''' [[File:Motherbound I wish I was a star.jpg|center|thumb|533x533px|I wish I was a star, I wish heaven wasnέΑβt bitter.]] The garden extended endlessly through the expanse of άΜδΆΩηάβΠΆΩδ. I look above, where I was once, and see how eternal their growth is. Their trunk is perpetual, their leaves are punished by a wind which blows from the east. Yet the leaves donέΑβt move. Yet the leaves donέΑβt care. They form eye-like patterns. Their gaze unnerves me. Mother said their were sacred. Her love truly belonged to them. '''My stars, how can I escape? My stars, how can I flee? From my mind, from the world, from mother. Your sapience is incomparable, your love is unconditional. There, what can I do then? I wish I could join you, be with you, yet adherence is what most I can attain. Please, your intelligence is what only can save me.''' I could hear that beautiful voice again. The symphony of their words resonated among the screeching earth and the scarlet trees. Angelical, extraordinary. A chorus of annexed voices, soothing like nothing I had ever listened: ''Do not cry, έΨΙέΨΙέΨΙέΨΙέΨΙέΨΙέΨΙέΨΙ, we hear your pleas.'' ''Flee to the east, there where her control wonέΑβt reach.'' ''It will be there, you can escape this world. '' ''Find the glowing sky and loosen her hold.'' '''The stars have given response, I shall travel to the east. There where I wonέΑβt need her anymore. Mother! I can flee from your grip, I can finally shape liberty. There where the sky glistens, where I can finally be. What will it be of you when your garden receives no more nourishment? Mother, will you finally value me? Or shall I leave you stranded behind, and recover my humanity? Mother, even in my darkest times, I still love you, but mother! Please, let me flee from this hell.''' '''As I traveled for eons, I finally found the glowing skies. The shimmering was unique. The stars were right, they must be, please, be this finally an escape to mother. Stars, stars, I adore, I love, please, come with me and flee this purgatory! Stars, I ascend, and see mother come. Her face with disgust, her garden judges. And as I touch the divine, the exit to my suffering, I hear their voice.''' But they sounded different, shady. They sounded likeέΑο mother. Mother, mother, mother! Why now, please let me flee! Mother, I suffer, I break but not die, I cry but I arenέΑβt heard. άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ άΩτάΧΦ╔ΝΆΩδάΩτ ΆηΗΆΩη ΆΩδΆΧ╖άβΖάΧΦάΨαΆΩδ άβΠάΟ╜ άΨαάΧΦ╔ΝάΧΦάΩτ─υΆΧ╖ΆΩδ. άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ άΩτάΧΦ╔ΝΆΩδάΩτ ΆηΗΆΩη άΩτ─υΆΧ╖╟ν╔ΝάΧΦάβΖΆΩδΆααΆγ╢╘╕╘╕άΟ╜ άΩτΆΩδάβΒάΟ╜ άβΠάΟ╜ ╘╕ΆΩηάΧΥΆΩδ. άβκάΜδάΟ╜ άΩτ─υάΩτ άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ άβΖΆΩηάβΠΆΩδ ─υάβΒ ΆηΗάΜδΆΩδ Άαα─υ╔ΝΆΧ╖ΆηΗ άΨα╘╕άΧΦάβΖΆΩδ? άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ ΆΧ╖ΆΩδάΧΦ╔ΝάβΖάΜδΆΩδάΩτ άΨα╔ΝΆΩηΆηΗΆΩδάβΖΆηΗ─υΆΩηάβΒ, άΜδΆΩδ╔ΝΆΩδ άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ άΧΦ╔ΝΆΩδ ╟ν╔ΝάΧΦάβΒΆηΗΆΩδάΩτ ΆΧ╖Άγ╢άβΖάΜδ. άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ ΆΧ╖ΆΩδάΧΦ╔ΝάβΖάΜδΆΩδάΩτ ─υάβΠάβΠΆΩη╔ΝΆηΗάΧΦ╘╕─υΆηΗάΟ╜, άΜδΆΩδ╔ΝΆΩδ άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ ΆααΆΩηΆγ╢άβΒάΩτ ─υΆηΗ. άβκάΜδάΟ╜ άΩτΆΩη άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ Άαα╘╕ΆΩδΆΩδ ΆηΗάΜδΆΩδάβΒ? άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢ άΧΦ╔ΝΆΩδ Άγ╢άβΒάβκΆΩη╔ΝΆηΗάΜδάΟ╜ ΆΩηΆαα άβΠάΟ╜ ╘╕ΆΩηάΧΥΆΩδ. άβΒΆΩηάβκ, άΧΦάβΖάβΖΆΩδάΨαΆηΗ ΆηΗάΜδΆΩδ άΩτΆΩδΆΧ╖ΆηΗ─υάβΒάΟ╜ ΆΩδάβΒΆααΆΩη╔ΝάβΖΆΩδάΩτ ΆηΗΆΩη άΟ╜ΆΩηΆγ╢. '''Mother, why, why, why! What had I done, what have you done? Why feast on my suffering, why give me a false illusion? Why build and break me on and on? Why care of the trees? You promised me I would be happy! Mother, I hate. I hate you, mother. I hate your paradise, your hell, your garden, your trees! Your wicked trees, all be killed! The earth pulsates and the skies laugh. I no longer want to kill, I no longer need your love. Mother, cease to be! And free this world from a torment.''' '''Mother, as your unending έΑεloveέΑζ sucked me inside, your lack of concern will spit me out! And I may fly among the skies of death, among the clouds of the eternal rest. YouέΑβll find another being, and another one, and another one! So I ask, when will you stop? When will it be enough? When there are no more things to feed your trees, what will you nourish them with?''' '''Mother, I am no longer bound to you.''' And her teeth pierced my flesh, my eyes were scooped out with her claws. As the world faded to a shade of green and red, I feel as those who I had given to mother. The sky was tainted with a dot of red. And then another, and another one appeared. The skies reached for my soul as I drowned among an hecatomb of leaves. Blood seeped from my body and flowers bloomed from my insides. As I am left in a state of gore, as I suffer like who had seen heaven but was denied, the sky crawls over my meats and digs in my body. Mother fluctuates endlessly and takes forms impossibly: necks frowned and wrists smelled. Mouthes connected and eyes swallowed it all. The earth cracked in half, and cracked again. The trees screamed and sang about the heavens, about hell, about the void, about geometry. The paradise broke perpetually and earth rose, voids descended and everything altered quantum between void and earth in a 50/50 ratio. When the perfection is interrupted by me, mother ceases to be, I cease to be, we cease to be. [[File:Motherbound broken paradise.jpeg|thumb|Mother, mother! We broke and ceased to be, my thoughts were pierced by your ideologies of a heaven!|center|530x530px]] '''The child canέΑβt live without the mother,''' '''The mother canέΑβt live without the child.''' '''It is then, when we are born,''' '''we are''' άβΠΆΩηΆηΗ'''άΜδ'''ΆΩδ╔ΝάΩ╣ΆΩηΆγ╢άβΒάΩτ'''.'''
